groups.requirements: Replace per-generator thread::spawn in generator.rs with a reusable worker thread. After ~130 sequential generator thread spawns on macOS aarch64, subsequent JIT code execution crashes with EXC_BAD_ACCESS code=257 (PAC failure). The crash is in the calling thread (not generator thread), thread counts stay at 2 (threads ARE joined), and persists even with mem::forget (no JIT page freeing). Root cause: cumulative pthread lifecycle operations on Apple Silicon corrupt process state. Fix: a long-lived worker thread receives (body_fn, args, channels) tuples and executes generator bodies sequentially, reusing the same thread. Key constraints: generator body uses thread_local GEN_TX/GEN_RX for yield communication — the worker thread must set these up per task. Shared capture buffer must be propagated to worker thread. Must support concurrent generators within a single test (multiple comprehensions).
questions: Should this be a single worker thread or a small pool (e.g., 2-4 threads)? Single thread means generators within one test run sequentially; pool allows parallel comprehensions.
Should the worker thread(s) be lazy-initialized on first generator creation, or eagerly at runtime init?
